Just after seeing it. First off, it HAS to be seen on the big screen to really appreciate the aerobatics when they are in those jets. IT IS SUCH AN ADRENALINE RUSH and it's just so freaking COOL!!! Secondly, did I mention how cool it is. Thirdly, it builds on the original without relying too heavily on nostalgia. For me the movie is Mavericks and Rooster/Goose even though Goose isn't there physically his presence is felt in the movie in a lovely, not shove it down your throat nod to the original. I got weirdly emotional when they went on their mission - like had proper tears coming down my face which I wasn't expecting. The addition of Val Kilmer is also a great moment. Tom Cruise and Jennifer Connolly have great chemistry. And finally, as someone who would always go to the theatre every weekend before COVID, James Bond and Batman didn't get be back into the theatre but Maverick did and it was well worth the price of admission. It's a terrific move that I would highly recommend that you go see (on the big screen).

You know every once and awhile a movie hits the big screen that really lives up to the hype. I will not be putting spoilers into my review. This is one ride you need to experience for yourselves.
What I can add is Tom Cruise and the every so lovely Jennifer Connelly as always their acting was superb. The supporting / other stars were also believable and the story is well written. It is touching , and exciting! It is quite easy to feel as if you are right there.
This shouldn't surprise anyone with the likes of Jerry Bruckheimer who is tops himself. And Tom Cruise over the years has fine tuned his acting. He has become a true master of his trade and a real professional who likes things done to where the experience is top rate for those watching. 
I will admit I was sold on this movie along time ago. With Tom Cruise and then you add stars like Val Kilmer ( we love ya man! ), Jennifer Connelly , Ed Harris and you have a serious amount of Hollywood royalty. And I refuse to sell the other actors and actresses short. All the actors/actresses in this movie performed well above expectations! 
If you are expecting another movie like the original Top Gun then you will be in for a new and amazing experience. 

If you feel the need for speed. This is one you do not want to miss! 
I am giving this movie a 10 myself. Only because there isn't anything above a 10.

Btw, It is always nice to see Ed Harris. I am glad he was involved. He is just one of those rare actors. As for the rest of the cast. I see very bright futures in store for them. 
Btw , I am going back to watch it again. 
Oh and the fact that Tom Cruise is an accomplished pilot most certainly helped!

SPOILER: Movie was good, Ice talked with help of a computer screen, and dies in the movie from reoccurance of cancer.  The main plot is the adversary is on the verge of operating a new uranium plant.  Maverick was called by Ice to teach the latest Top Gun grads.  Goose’s son hates Mav.  They train and train, and when Ice died, Mav was pulled out if this mission.  But Mav “stole” his F18 to prove the mission can be accomplished below the standard alloted time.  They go on the mission, blows up the adversary’s secret plant.  Then they all had to do some fancy flying against SAMs and MIGs.  Mav’s plane was hit and he was presumably dead.  The remaining team flys home, except for Gooseks son (Rooster) who searched for him and saved Mav from certain death from enemy helicopter.  Rooster who disobeyed orders to return, shows up and blows up the helicopter while simultaneously being hit by a missle.  Now both Rooster and Mav are in enemy territory.  They found an old F14, started it up, and took off.  Fought off the first enemy but had to contend with the 2nd MIG.  Of which Hangman comes in from nowhere and splashes the MIG.  They all fly home onboard aircraft carrier safe.  Mav gets Penny Benjamin and they live happily ever after since that was Mav’s last mission by the Navy.  This storyline is very similar to Star Wars where the team had to blow up the Deathstar with its one vulnerability.  When the enemy was about to wipe out the main character,  only to be saved by a team mate (Han Solo).  Great scenes… definitely see it at the theater!

Just watched it, its good. VERY logical story line.  more over they did all the things they REALLY needed to do to make this work.  They brought in the original film main editors (who btw are truly responsible for the success of the original film because the way they cut the film and did audio pickups to make it work was NOT the story Tony Scott made as he only had an idea and not a solid story.    The film is certainly good,  but is it better than the original ....no..  but thats not to say its bad.  its not bad at all,  and its a proper sequel that shouldve been made decades ago.  Thankfully the logic of the story still hold weight even with Tom Cruise being much older.   On a ranking 1-10 ide give it an 8.   in alphabetic rankings.. B+.   Where the original film excells is its rollercoaster ride of a hard driving score along side fast paced visuals.   Also,  contrary to all the talk about real in camera footage,  in actuallity the original film has alot, and even more dynamic shots than this new film.  Really re-watch the original film and look for all the real aeronautic shots.. there is lots of them though majority of the pilot dialog stuff is on a little set with  rear projection .

It’s rare that I get excited for a movie event…or at least excited enough to write about it.  The year was 1986.  I was 15 years old.  I secured my very first job working a DOD military dependent internship at the AIMD jet shop assisting Navy in Cubi Point.  I was the “grease monkey” assistant to those tasked with the repair and maintenance of the TF-41 engine-- the powerplant of the legendary F14 Tomcat.  I was moved to choose this job based on events of that year.  Watching from my High School auditorium on April 14, 1986, President Ronald Reagan addressed the nation regarding the swift and decisive action against the terrorist regime of Khaddafi.  One month later, the original TOP GUN movie was released. Watching it in the theater on the US Naval Base in Subic Bay, together with my HS buddies…it was something else!  We were proud of our Navy roots and proud of our President for his assertiveness.  Proud of the US Servicemen and women who carried out justice and of our Navy families.  It was a different time, different place…a different age.  Tonight, 36 years later, I am excited to finally see the premiere of the sequel to my favorite movie of all time!
